title: Pyrus × tomentosa
taxonomy: Pyrus × tomentosa is a hybrid species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It is a cross between Pyrus communis and Pyrus elaeagrifolia. This hybrid is commonly known as the 'Tomentosa' pear. The classification of Pyrus × tomentosa places it within the genus Pyrus, which includes other species of pear trees. The exact classification may vary depending on the specific botanical authority.
uses: Pyrus × tomentosa, also known as the weeping pear, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. The bark and leaves of the tree are said to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate conditions such as arthritis. Additionally, the plant's extracts have been studied for their antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, which could contribute to its potential use in treating infections and promoting wound healing. The plant's leaves and flowers have also been used to treat various digestive issues, including diarrhea and stomach ulcers. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential benefits of Pyrus × tomentosa.

The bark and leaves of the tree contain flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Studies have shown that extracts from Pyrus × tomentosa may have potential in treating conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. Additionally, the tree's leaves and stems have been used to treat respiratory problems, including coughs and colds. intro: Pyrus × tomentosa, also known as the downy pear, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that is a hybrid of Pyrus communis and Pyrus pashia. The bark and leaves of the downy pear have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Pyrus × tomentosa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with the downy pear include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat colds, sore throats, and respiratory issues.
